# UC-13.7.11 · Fluentd Tag Routing Misconfiguration — Unmatched Records and Null-Route Detection

> **Criticality:** Medium &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Configuration, Reliability &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We watch the little route names on log streams so when the collector stops sending them down the right path, we notice quickly. We catch quiet discards into the digital trash bin before anyone assumes an app went silent. We help you spot brand-new stream names that nobody planned for.*

---

## Description

Detects Fluentd tag taxonomy drift and routing misconfiguration where events miss explicit match stanzas, fall through star-star rescue paths, land in null outputs, or disappear when disable_router suppresses routing, distinct from buffer throughput, worker crashes, memory pressure, Fluent Bit flush latency, filter-only drops, filesystem backlog, TLS faults, or multiline parser stuck states. The correlation joins supervisor warnings, Prometheus router no-match counters, optional Fluent Bit input-versus-drop ratios, HTTP event collector arrival lanes, CIM Change activity on collector hosts, and a rolling fluentd_tag_inventory.csv baseline so first-seen tags, tag-to-output skew, and accidental catch-all ordering surface before dashboards imply healthy pipelines.

## Value

Executive and platform stakeholders retain defensible evidence that logging completeness reflects intentional routing, not silent discard into null sinks or mis-ordered match blocks, which protects audit narratives for regulated workloads and prevents multi-day hunts that blame applications for missing telemetry when the collector taxonomy drifted first.

## Implementation

Land fluentd:log warnings on index=fluent, fluentd:metrics and prometheus scrape series on index=otel_metrics, fluentbit:metrics for drop-to-input ratios, httpevent arrival on index=fluent, and accelerated CIM Change summaries; maintain fluentd_tag_inventory.csv for seven-day baselines. Schedule observability_uc_13_7_11_fluentd_tag_routing_drift every fifteen minutes with earliest=-24h@h. Tune severity after two sprint cycles, throttle duplicate merge_key pages for thirty minutes, and route critical alerts to platform logging on-call with Git-backed diff links.

## Evidence

Saved search observability_uc_13_7_11_fluentd_tag_routing_drift with fifteen minute schedule; dashboard drilldowns on multisearch lanes; weekly fluentd_tag_inventory.csv export archived to a restricted evidence index.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

In a lab logging namespace, reorder match stanzas so a known tag falls through to a null output, confirm fluentd:log warnings, fluentd_input_router_no_match_total increases against baseline, and observability_uc_13_7_11_fluentd_tag_routing_drift yields critical or high within thirty minutes.

### Negative scenario

After restoring correct match order, reloading Fluentd, refreshing fluentd_tag_inventory.csv baselines, and validating Prometheus scrape health, confirm severity returns to low noise and filtering to critical plus high yields zero rows for thirty consecutive runs.

## Detailed Implementation

### Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with the observability pipeline team that operates Fluentd DaemonSets, sidecars, and bare-metal collectors, the Kubernetes platform group that owns namespace labels and admission policies, network and syslog teams that see facility rotations, and Splunk administrators who land agent logs, Prometheus scrape indexes, and HTTP event collector arrival lanes. This use case isolates tag taxonomy drift and routing misconfiguration in Fluentd: events whose tags no longer align with explicit match stanzas, records that fall through to a star-star rescue route, events intentionally or accidentally sent to a null output, streams suppressed when disable_router appears in generated config, and rescue handling under label ERROR that can mask volume if operators treat the lane as noise. The angle is deliberately not UC-13.7.1 raw input or output throughput collapse, not UC-13.7.2 retry storms, not UC-13.7.3 disk or network I/O saturation, not UC-13.7.4 worker crash loops, not UC-13.7.5 memory buffer pressure, not UC-13.7.6 Fluent Bit flush latency service levels, not UC-13.7.7 filter drop percentages in isolation, not UC-13.7.8 filesystem storage backlog, not UC-13.7.9 Transport Layer Security handshake failures, and not UC-13.7.10 multiline parser stuck states. Here the failure mode is structural routing: a new Kubernetes namespace string, a rewritten pod label used inside tag fragments, a changed syslog facility integer, a container runtime prefix on source tags, or a GitOps merge that reorders catch-all match blocks so benign traffic silently disappears into null sinks while dashboards still show healthy buffer metrics.

Understanding Fluentd routing requires reading match specificity, label branching, and the default forward path when nothing matches. Operator logs emit pattern-not-matched class warnings, while fluent-plugin-prometheus exposes counters such as fluentd_input_router_no_match_total on modern builds. Rescue labels and null outputs are valid during testing yet catastrophic when left in production Helm values. Hybrid estates that run Fluent Bit ahead of Fluentd should compare input record totals against filter drop counters as a secondary hint, not as a one-to-one substitute, because Bit filters drop for reasons outside tag routing. Splunk-side validation contrasts HTTP event collector arrival counts against upstream tag cardinalities when teams claim complete ingestion despite router gaps.

Risk briefing: canary clusters and short-lived namespaces create first-seen tags that resemble incidents until baselines refresh. Planned migrations from docker log tags to containerd formats will shift prefixes for days. Legal and privacy reviewers should confirm whether raw tag strings include customer identifiers before exposing them in shared dashboards. Break-glass roles may need wider access to fluentd:log indexes than general analysts receive.

RBAC and safety: service accounts that scrape Prometheus endpoints require read-only credentials. Jobs that export configuration snapshots need narrowly scoped Kubernetes read permissions rather than interactive cluster-admin tokens. Change windows should stamp ticket identifiers onto related events so Splunk timelines align with approved rollouts.

Differentiation recap: unmatched match stanzas, null-route and disable_router behavior, label ERROR rescue visibility, tag inventory drift versus a seven-day baseline in fluentd_tag_inventory.csv, Prometheus router no-match counters, optional Fluent Bit drop ratio hints, Splunk HTTP event collector arrival cross-checks, and Splunk Common Information Model Change summaries for configuration hosts—not buffer byte rates alone, not multiline merge health, not TLS error text.

### Step 2 — Configure data collection

Stand up five multisearch arms Splunk can fuse without assuming a single telemetry lane always works during incidents.

Fluentd supervisor log arm: forward Fluentd own logs into index=fluent with sourcetype=fluentd:log. Capture host, cluster, kubernetes namespace annotations when the collector runs in Kubernetes, and raw lines that include pattern-not-matched warnings, record-suppressed language, disable_router mentions, and label ERROR stack traces. Normalize UTC timestamps and strip ANSI color. Map kubernetes.pod_name into host when bare host is missing.

Fluentd Prometheus exporter arm: scrape fluent-plugin-prometheus endpoints into index=otel_metrics with sourcetype=fluentd:metrics or sourcetype=prometheus depending on your OpenTelemetry Collector mapping. Preserve labels for instance, pod, namespace, job, and any tag cardinality labels your chart emits. Required series include fluentd_input_router_no_match_total when exposed; alias vendor-specific names at ingest. Record scrape timestamps faithfully so rate calculations remain honest during scrape outages.

Fluent Bit ratio arm: land fluentbit:metrics in index=otel_metrics with flb_input_records_total and flb_filter_records_dropped_total or vendor equivalents. Keep cluster and host labels aligned with the Fluentd arms for join keys. This arm is supportive: elevated drops after label_selector changes can indicate silent exclusion before Splunk ever sees a tag.

Splunk HTTP event collector arm: land tokenized or sourcetype-tagged httpevent streams into index=fluent (or your governed application index) with cluster and namespace fields extracted at ingest. This arm validates that bytes still arrive after routing fixes; it does not prove semantic correctness of each tag without content sampling.

Splunk Common Information Model Change arm: accelerate Change summaries for hosts and objects tied to logging configuration. The search uses tstats against datamodel=Change with nodename=Change so configuration edits on collectors correlate with sudden tag bursts; post-filter in dashboards for objects whose names reference fluentd or kubernetes when noise requires narrowing.

Maintain fluentd_tag_inventory.csv with columns tag_pattern, cluster, expected_output_lane, baseline_7d_seen, owner_team, notes, and first_seen_epoch. Refresh weekly from automated tag cardinality jobs or from saved searches that list rare tags. Sister datacenter pairs should carry matching rows to avoid false drift when one region legitimately omits a workload.

Example operator helpers for design reviews:

kubectl -n logging get configmap fluentd-config -o yaml and search for match, label, and null output stanzas.
curl against the local Prometheus scrape port for fluentd_input_router_no_match_total and confirm labels match kube state.
Compare helm release history timestamps to Splunk Change.dest rows that reference the same nodes.

props.conf guidance: TRUNCATE must accommodate long tag strings in raw warnings. LINE_BREAKER should keep multiline stack traces from label ERROR intact. FIELDALIAS maps should unify dotted Prometheus labels with flattened forms.

Pre-save validation: each arm must return events in lab within one hour of enabling collection. Induce a harmless unmatched tag in non-production, confirm fluentd:log warnings appear, metrics counters move, Fluent Bit ratios shift within expected bounds, httpevent counts remain non-zero for healthy tokens, and tstats returns Change rows after a controlled ConfigMap edit.

### Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as observability_uc_13_7_11_fluentd_tag_routing_drift with a fifteen minute schedule during steady-state production, dispatch earliest=-24h@h, dispatch latest=now, and alert when severity is critical or high after tuning for fleet noise. Throttle duplicate cluster, host, fluentd_tag tuples for thirty minutes unless severity escalates. Include recommended_action tokens in pager bodies so incident commanders open runbooks without rerunning ad hoc searches.

Pipeline narrative: multisearch fans Fluentd supervisor logs, Fluentd Prometheus counters, Fluent Bit input versus drop counters, Splunk HTTP event collector arrivals, and Change datamodel summaries so a silent single lane cannot hide a genuine taxonomy crisis. coalesce() ladders absorb field naming drift across exporters and Kubernetes labels. streamstats builds rolling sums of log-derived unmatched hits per merge key. eventstats adds per-cluster medians for router no-match counters and for Fluent Bit input and drop baselines. case() maps combined signals into critical, high, medium, and low tiers. inputlookup joins enrich tag rows with expected_output_lane, baseline_7d_seen, and owner_team for governance overlays.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.7.11 Fluentd tag routing drift unmatched match stanzas null rescue HEC cross-check; tunables earliest=-24h@h")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=fluent sourcetype=fluentd:log earliest=-24h@h latest=now ("pattern not matched" OR "no patterns matched" OR "record is suppressed" OR disable_router OR "@ERROR")
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,k8s_cluster_name,kubernetes_cluster,"unknown_cluster"))))
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,hostname,splunk_server,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval k8s_ns=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(kubernetes_namespace,k8s_namespace,namespace,""))))
      | rex field=_raw max_match=1 "(?i)tag[=:]\\s*(?<tag_rex>[\\w\\.\\-\\/]+)"
      | eval fluentd_tag=trim(toString(coalesce(tag,fluentd_tag,record_tag,tag_rex,"__unparsed__")))
      | eval lane="fluentd_supervisor_log"
      | eval no_match_hits=1
      | eval router_no_match_total=null()
      | eval fb_inp=null()
      | eval fb_drop=null()
      | eval hec_ingest_count=null()
      | fields _time cluster host k8s_ns fluentd_tag lane no_match_hits router_no_match_total fb_inp fb_drop hec_ingest_count ]
    [ search index=otel_metrics (sourcetype=fluentd:metrics OR sourcetype=prometheus) earliest=-24h@h latest=now ("fluentd_input_router_no_match" OR router_no_match OR no_match_total)
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,k8s_cluster_name,dc,"unknown_cluster"))))
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(instance,host,pod,kubernetes_pod_name,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval k8s_ns=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(kubernetes_namespace,namespace,""))))
      | eval fluentd_tag=trim(toString(coalesce(tag,exported_tag,lab_tag,"metric_cardinality")))
      | eval router_no_match_total=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(fluentd_input_router_no_match_total,metric_value,"0")),10)
      | eval lane="fluentd_prometheus_exporter"
      | eval no_match_hits=null()
      | eval fb_inp=null()
      | eval fb_drop=null()
      | eval hec_ingest_count=null()
      | fields _time cluster host k8s_ns fluentd_tag lane no_match_hits router_no_match_total fb_inp fb_drop hec_ingest_count ]
    [ search index=otel_metrics sourcetype=fluentbit:metrics earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,k8s_cluster_name,"unknown_cluster"))))
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,kubernetes.node_name,pod_name,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval k8s_ns=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(kubernetes_namespace,namespace,""))))
      | eval fluentd_tag="fluent_bit_input_filter_ratio"
      | eval fb_inp=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(flb_input_records_total,fluentbit_input_records_total,input_records_total,"0")),10)
      | eval fb_drop=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(flb_filter_records_dropped_total,fluentbit_filter_records_dropped_total,"0")),10)
      | eval lane="fluent_bit_prom_equivalent"
      | eval no_match_hits=null()
      | eval router_no_match_total=null()
      | eval hec_ingest_count=null()
      | fields _time cluster host k8s_ns fluentd_tag lane no_match_hits router_no_match_total fb_inp fb_drop hec_ingest_count ]
    [ search index=fluent sourcetype=httpevent earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,dc,"unknown_cluster"))))
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,splunk_server,"hec_indexer_host"))))
      | eval k8s_ns=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(namespace,k8s_ns,""))))
      | eval fluentd_tag=trim(toString(coalesce(source_tag,tag,"hec_arrival_lane")))
      | eval hec_ingest_count=1
      | eval lane="splunk_hec_ingest"
      | eval no_match_hits=null()
      | eval router_no_match_total=null()
      | eval fb_inp=null()
      | eval fb_drop=null()
      | fields _time cluster host k8s_ns fluentd_tag lane no_match_hits router_no_match_total fb_inp fb_drop hec_ingest_count ]
    [ | tstats summariesonly=f latest(_time) AS _time count AS chg_cnt latest(Change.object) AS chg_object latest(Change.user) AS change_user FROM datamodel=Change WHERE nodename=Change earliest=-7d@d latest=now BY Change.dest
      | rename Change.dest AS host
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(host)))
      | eval cluster="cim_change_inventory_lane"
      | eval k8s_ns=""
      | eval fluentd_tag="config_change_fingerprint"
      | eval lane="cim_change_tstats"
      | eval no_match_hits=null()
      | eval router_no_match_total=chg_cnt
      | eval fb_inp=null()
      | eval fb_drop=null()
      | eval hec_ingest_count=null()
      | fields _time cluster host k8s_ns fluentd_tag lane no_match_hits router_no_match_total fb_inp fb_drop hec_ingest_count chg_object change_user ]
| eval merge_key=strcat(cluster,"|",host,"|",coalesce(fluentd_tag,"__none__"))
| streamstats window=36 global=f sum(no_match_hits) AS rolling_nm BY merge_key
| eventstats median(router_no_match_total) AS rnm_p50 stdev(router_no_match_total) AS rnm_sd BY cluster
| eventstats median(fb_inp) AS fb_inp_p50 median(fb_drop) AS fb_drop_p50 BY cluster lane
| join type=left max=0 fluentd_tag cluster
    [| inputlookup fluentd_tag_inventory.csv
     | eval fluentd_tag=trim(toString(tag_pattern))
     | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(cluster)))
     | eval baseline_7d_seen=trim(toString(baseline_7d_seen))
     | fields fluentd_tag cluster expected_output_lane baseline_7d_seen owner_team ]
| eval baseline_seen_flag=if(isnotnull(expected_output_lane),1,0)
| eval new_tag_flag=if(baseline_7d_seen=="false" OR baseline_7d_seen=="0" OR isnull(baseline_7d_seen),1,0)
| eval severity=case(new_tag_flag==1 AND match(lane,"fluentd_supervisor") AND coalesce(rolling_nm,0)>=5,"critical", coalesce(rolling_nm,0)>=50 OR coalesce(router_no_match_total,0)>=40,"high", coalesce(fb_drop,0)>0 AND coalesce(fb_inp_p50,0)>0 AND fb_drop>(fb_inp_p50*0.08),"high", coalesce(rnm_sd,0)>0 AND coalesce(router_no_match_total,0)>coalesce(rnm_p50,0)*2,"medium", true(),"low")
| eval recommended_action=case(severity=="critical","rewire_match_stanzas_rescue_label_error_and_git_diff", severity=="high","inspect_null_output_disable_router_and_catch_all_order", severity=="medium","reconcile_fluent_bit_drop_ratio_to_tag_taxonomy", true(),"refresh_fluentd_tag_inventory_baseline_weekly")
| table _time cluster host k8s_ns fluentd_tag lane rolling_nm router_no_match_total fb_inp fb_drop hec_ingest_count severity recommended_action baseline_seen_flag owner_team rnm_p50 new_tag_flag merge_key
```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

cron_schedule = */15 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -24h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch observability_uc_13_7_11_fluentd_tag_routing_drift | where severity IN ("critical","high")

Performance: if Job Inspector shows multisearch imbalance, materialize each arm hourly into a summary index keyed on merge_key, widen alert searches on summaries, and retain this full search for investigations. Cap wildcard volume on httpevent with production index allowlists after security agrees you are not dropping rare evidence rows.

Dashboard design: host a matrix by cluster and fluentd_tag with severity-colored rows, sparklines of rolling_nm, drilldowns to fluentd:log excerpts, otel_metrics labels, fluentbit:metrics ratios, httpevent samples, and Change object text for the same host.

### Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth begins on the suspect collector. Compare live curl totals for fluentd_input_router_no_match_total to the Splunk row for the same instance label inside the last two scrape windows. When counters disagree, verify stale pod labels after restarts and duplicate scrape jobs before blaming config.

Cross-check configuration: print active match order from the live process, then diff Git for catch-all star-star blocks placed ahead of specific routes. When Git shows a fix but Splunk still shows drift, suspect incomplete reload or two ConfigMaps mounted to different containers.

Downstream validation: pick a critical severity row, open httpevent samples in Splunk, and confirm timestamps continue while fluentd:log shows warnings for the same tag. Pair with sister datacenter traffic: when only one region spikes, diff fluentd_tag_inventory.csv rows and chart versions.

Synthetic validation: in a lab namespace, inject a tag that no longer matches any stanza, confirm observability_uc_13_7_11_fluentd_tag_routing_drift returns high within two evaluation windows, restore ordering, reload, and confirm severity returns to low.

Negative test: on a healthy cluster after inventory refresh, filter severity to critical and high and expect zero rows for thirty consecutive runs when thresholds match your calibrated noise floor.

Runbook linkage: document expected warning bursts during single-namespace canary deploys so validators do not file false defects when change calendars show an approved rollout.

### Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — New Kubernetes namespace after a label rewrite breaks a pod-name regular expression inside match: open fluentd_tag_inventory.csv, confirm baseline_7d_seen flipped false, diff Git for the match block, and roll a corrected pattern under change control.

Case 2 — Application rotates syslog facility code so tags derived from facility no longer match legacy stanzas: correlate syslog forwarding timelines, update match fragments, and revalidate Prometheus counters plus httpevent arrival for the facility owner.

Case 3 — Container runtime tag prefix changes when nodes move from docker to containerd: compare cluster node images, adjust rewrite rules, and refresh inventory baselines after the migration window closes.

Case 4 — label_selector or Kubernetes metadata filter updates silently exclude pods: inspect Fluent Bit drop counters alongside fluentd:log warnings, reconcile selectors with namespace allow lists, and document intentional exclusions.

Case 5 — Accidental star-star catch-all ordered before specific routes sends traffic to a null output: audit match order, move specific stanzas above rescue blocks, and verify label ERROR volume drops.

Case 6 — Parser drift rewrites records into unexpected tag strings: diff parser sections against sample payloads, replay fixtures through a lab Fluentd, and align prometheus label cardinality after fixes.

Case 7 — Multi-line or out_forward aggregator emits a new intermediate tag operators forgot to route: trace upstream forward paths, add explicit match for the aggregator tag, and confirm downstream httpevent fields.

Case 8 — log4j2 layout adds a prefix that shifts tag extraction assumptions: partner with application owners, update rewrite rules, and extend inventory notes with the new layout version.

Case 9 — Helm chart defaults enable disable_router during a bad merge: search fluentd:log for disable_router tokens, revert chart values, and rehearse code review gates for logging charts.

Case 10 — Rescue label ERROR absorbs volume operators mute in syslog: restore alerting on ERROR lanes, ensure Splunk routing for those events, and separate testing rescues from production labels.

Case 11 — Prometheus scrape misses mimic flat counters: validate target health, compare scrape interval skew, and dedupe replica scrapes before rewriting Fluentd config.

Case 12 — Inventory CSV stale expected_output_lane causes constant medium severity: refresh baselines from fourteen-day medians per cluster, document seasonal workload shifts, and automate replication from configuration management databases.

Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the spl field exactly; multisearch lists five arms including fluent sourcetype=fluentd:log, otel_metrics fluentd or prometheus scrape lanes, otel_metrics fluentbit:metrics, fluent sourcetype=httpevent, and tstats Change; coalesce, streamstats, eventstats, case, inputlookup, and join appear; final table lists eighteen analyst-facing columns including recommended_action and merge_key.

Extended platform narrative for fleet operators: treat fluentd_tag_inventory.csv rows as first-class configuration items alongside network policies. When platform engineers promote a new logging chart, require a pull request that updates baseline_7d_seen using a seven-day trailing cardinality job to avoid alert thrash. Document ordering rules for match stanzas in the same repository that stores application defaults so catch-all blocks cannot merge ahead of specific routes without review. For hybrid estates, annotate inventory notes with whether Fluent Bit or Fluentd owns tag finalization to prevent duplicate rewrite rules fighting each other.

Version governance: maintain rolling minimum chart versions per cluster and fail change pipelines when a region drifts behind a reference region without a recorded exception. Pair release-note review with this control so router behavior changes are not lost in routine image bumps. When vendor distributions lag upstream Fluentd, annotate inventory notes with distribution-specific identifiers to prevent false confidence during audits.

Downstream Splunk parsing: intermediate forwarders must not strip tag fields that investigations rely on. When this search shows healthy metrics yet analysts cannot find raw warnings, move investigation to intermediate tiers and mirror evidence in the configuration management database for forwarders. Keep httpevent field extractions consistent across clusters so the arrival arm does not silently empty during schema migrations.

Governance overlays: stamp change_ticket_id on configuration snapshot events when Helm values change match order. Archive weekly snapshots of fluentd_tag_inventory.csv to a restricted evidence index for auditors who require ownership traceability. Pair alert outputs with pipeline_region labels so large enterprises route pages to regional squads without manual lookup.

Advanced forensics: when rolling_nm oscillates with batch schedules, separate service identities for cron-driven namespaces using role labels in fluentd_tag_inventory.csv rather than muting the entire cluster. For ephemeral pods that appear briefly, ensure tag cardinality jobs run frequently enough to capture first-seen strings before baselines misclassify them as long-term drift.

Capacity footnote: this control intentionally does not optimize search cost. If multisearch runtime exceeds service level objectives, pre-aggregate otel_metrics into fifteen-minute summary indexes keyed on cluster, host, and fluentd_tag while retaining raw arms for investigations. Security isolation: restrict httpevent indexes to roles that require payload content; metrics-only roles can retain otel_metrics and internal Change arms for operational dashboards without reading application bodies.

Collaboration with developers: require service owners to publish sample tag strings whenever they change logging frameworks or metadata labels. Insert those samples into runbooks linked from fluentd_tag_inventory.csv so regex authors regression-test against real strings rather than abbreviated documentation excerpts.

Closing operational guidance: rehearse this saved search quarterly with tabletop exercises that include observability, Kubernetes platform, and representative application leads. Rotate runbook URLs when teams reorganize so pager deep links do not rot. Keep a single source of truth for sister datacenter pairing labels to prevent mistaken cross-region comparisons during partial failovers.

Platform hygiene addendum: document how star-star rescue interacts with store_null and copy directives so junior engineers do not assume rescue always means lossless buffering. Capture explicit screenshots of Grafana or Splunk dashboards that plot fluentd_input_router_no_match_total per instance so executives receive familiar visuals during reviews. When service meshes inject sidecars that rewrite metadata, add mesh revision columns to inventory CSVs to explain sudden tag bursts without invoking security incidents.

Evidence retention stance: keep ninety days of fluentd:log warnings in compliance with corporate log retention policy, and shorter hot retention for high-volume httpevent indexes if legal approves summarization for this control. Ensure backup jobs include fluentd_tag_inventory.csv history so accidental spreadsheet edits can be reconstructed during audits.

Training narrative: onboarding modules should walk new engineers through a single end-to-end example where a misplaced catch-all star-star stanza demonstrably sends customer traffic to a null sink, shows the prometheus counter move, and restores visibility after reordering stanzas. Practical exercises beat abstract documentation for this failure mode.

Final assurance statement: this card is written so an engineer who has never touched Fluentd can still follow the arms, interpret severity, and open the correct Git directory within one incident rotation, provided inventory hygiene and RBAC prerequisites are satisfied.


## SPL

```spl
`comment("UC-13.7.11 Fluentd tag routing drift unmatched match stanzas null rescue HEC cross-check; tunables earliest=-24h@h")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=fluent sourcetype=fluentd:log earliest=-24h@h latest=now ("pattern not matched" OR "no patterns matched" OR "record is suppressed" OR disable_router OR "@ERROR")
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,k8s_cluster_name,kubernetes_cluster,"unknown_cluster"))))
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,hostname,splunk_server,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval k8s_ns=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(kubernetes_namespace,k8s_namespace,namespace,""))))
      | rex field=_raw max_match=1 "(?i)tag[=:]\\s*(?<tag_rex>[\\w\\.\\-\\/]+)"
      | eval fluentd_tag=trim(toString(coalesce(tag,fluentd_tag,record_tag,tag_rex,"__unparsed__")))
      | eval lane="fluentd_supervisor_log"
      | eval no_match_hits=1
      | eval router_no_match_total=null()
      | eval fb_inp=null()
      | eval fb_drop=null()
      | eval hec_ingest_count=null()
      | fields _time cluster host k8s_ns fluentd_tag lane no_match_hits router_no_match_total fb_inp fb_drop hec_ingest_count ]
    [ search index=otel_metrics (sourcetype=fluentd:metrics OR sourcetype=prometheus) earliest=-24h@h latest=now ("fluentd_input_router_no_match" OR router_no_match OR no_match_total)
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,k8s_cluster_name,dc,"unknown_cluster"))))
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(instance,host,pod,kubernetes_pod_name,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval k8s_ns=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(kubernetes_namespace,namespace,""))))
      | eval fluentd_tag=trim(toString(coalesce(tag,exported_tag,lab_tag,"metric_cardinality")))
      | eval router_no_match_total=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(fluentd_input_router_no_match_total,metric_value,"0")),10)
      | eval lane="fluentd_prometheus_exporter"
      | eval no_match_hits=null()
      | eval fb_inp=null()
      | eval fb_drop=null()
      | eval hec_ingest_count=null()
      | fields _time cluster host k8s_ns fluentd_tag lane no_match_hits router_no_match_total fb_inp fb_drop hec_ingest_count ]
    [ search index=otel_metrics sourcetype=fluentbit:metrics earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,k8s_cluster_name,"unknown_cluster"))))
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,kubernetes.node_name,pod_name,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval k8s_ns=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(kubernetes_namespace,namespace,""))))
      | eval fluentd_tag="fluent_bit_input_filter_ratio"
      | eval fb_inp=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(flb_input_records_total,fluentbit_input_records_total,input_records_total,"0")),10)
      | eval fb_drop=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(flb_filter_records_dropped_total,fluentbit_filter_records_dropped_total,"0")),10)
      | eval lane="fluent_bit_prom_equivalent"
      | eval no_match_hits=null()
      | eval router_no_match_total=null()
      | eval hec_ingest_count=null()
      | fields _time cluster host k8s_ns fluentd_tag lane no_match_hits router_no_match_total fb_inp fb_drop hec_ingest_count ]
    [ search index=fluent sourcetype=httpevent earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,dc,"unknown_cluster"))))
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,splunk_server,"hec_indexer_host"))))
      | eval k8s_ns=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(namespace,k8s_ns,""))))
      | eval fluentd_tag=trim(toString(coalesce(source_tag,tag,"hec_arrival_lane")))
      | eval hec_ingest_count=1
      | eval lane="splunk_hec_ingest"
      | eval no_match_hits=null()
      | eval router_no_match_total=null()
      | eval fb_inp=null()
      | eval fb_drop=null()
      | fields _time cluster host k8s_ns fluentd_tag lane no_match_hits router_no_match_total fb_inp fb_drop hec_ingest_count ]
    [ | tstats summariesonly=f latest(_time) AS _time count AS chg_cnt latest(Change.object) AS chg_object latest(Change.user) AS change_user FROM datamodel=Change WHERE nodename=Change earliest=-7d@d latest=now BY Change.dest
      | rename Change.dest AS host
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(host)))
      | eval cluster="cim_change_inventory_lane"
      | eval k8s_ns=""
      | eval fluentd_tag="config_change_fingerprint"
      | eval lane="cim_change_tstats"
      | eval no_match_hits=null()
      | eval router_no_match_total=chg_cnt
      | eval fb_inp=null()
      | eval fb_drop=null()
      | eval hec_ingest_count=null()
      | fields _time cluster host k8s_ns fluentd_tag lane no_match_hits router_no_match_total fb_inp fb_drop hec_ingest_count chg_object change_user ]
| eval merge_key=strcat(cluster,"|",host,"|",coalesce(fluentd_tag,"__none__"))
| streamstats window=36 global=f sum(no_match_hits) AS rolling_nm BY merge_key
| eventstats median(router_no_match_total) AS rnm_p50 stdev(router_no_match_total) AS rnm_sd BY cluster
| eventstats median(fb_inp) AS fb_inp_p50 median(fb_drop) AS fb_drop_p50 BY cluster lane
| join type=left max=0 fluentd_tag cluster
    [| inputlookup fluentd_tag_inventory.csv
     | eval fluentd_tag=trim(toString(tag_pattern))
     | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(cluster)))
     | eval baseline_7d_seen=trim(toString(baseline_7d_seen))
     | fields fluentd_tag cluster expected_output_lane baseline_7d_seen owner_team ]
| eval baseline_seen_flag=if(isnotnull(expected_output_lane),1,0)
| eval new_tag_flag=if(baseline_7d_seen=="false" OR baseline_7d_seen=="0" OR isnull(baseline_7d_seen),1,0)
| eval severity=case(new_tag_flag==1 AND match(lane,"fluentd_supervisor") AND coalesce(rolling_nm,0)>=5,"critical", coalesce(rolling_nm,0)>=50 OR coalesce(router_no_match_total,0)>=40,"high", coalesce(fb_drop,0)>0 AND coalesce(fb_inp_p50,0)>0 AND fb_drop>(fb_inp_p50*0.08),"high", coalesce(rnm_sd,0)>0 AND coalesce(router_no_match_total,0)>coalesce(rnm_p50,0)*2,"medium", true(),"low")
| eval recommended_action=case(severity=="critical","rewire_match_stanzas_rescue_label_error_and_git_diff", severity=="high","inspect_null_output_disable_router_and_catch_all_order", severity=="medium","reconcile_fluent_bit_drop_ratio_to_tag_taxonomy", true(),"refresh_fluentd_tag_inventory_baseline_weekly")
| table _time cluster host k8s_ns fluentd_tag lane rolling_nm router_no_match_total fb_inp fb_drop hec_ingest_count severity recommended_action baseline_seen_flag owner_team rnm_p50 new_tag_flag merge_key
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Performance.cpu_load_percent) AS cpu_load latest(Performance.mem_used_pct) AS mem_used count FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance.All_Performance earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Performance.host
| rename Performance.host AS perf_host
| eval perf_host=lower(trim(toString(perf_host)))
| table perf_host cpu_load mem_used count
```

## Visualization

Treemap of cluster by fluentd_tag volume, sortable table of rolling_nm and router_no_match_total with cell coloring by severity, single-value delta versus prior week for new_tag_flag rate, timeline of httpevent arrival alongside warning counts, and Change overlay strip for collector hosts.

## Known False Positives

Short-lived batch namespaces emit first-seen tags that look like drift until fluentd_tag_inventory.csv refreshes; correlate owner_team and change calendars before paging. Intentional null outputs in non-production clusters generate warnings that are policy-approved; segregate indexes or cluster labels so alerts scope to production. Prometheus scrape outages flatten fluentd_input_router_no_match_total derivatives without config edits; validate target up before opening routing tickets. Planned syslog facility migrations create temporary spikes; read notes column in the inventory lookup. Duplicate scrapers double-count counters and exaggerate router metrics until dedupe rules land. Debug verbosity on Fluentd raises benign pattern-not-matched noise during hot reloads; compare rolling_nm only after reload windows close. HTTP event collector tokens that fan out to multiple indexes can mask per-tag loss when this search still sees aggregate arrivals; sample content by tag when disputes arise. CIM Change arms include unrelated host activity; dashboard filters for fluentd objects reduce clutter. Canary pods that restart frequently produce tag_rex parse fallbacks; validate kubernetes metadata fields before treating __unparsed__ as production drift. Seasonal traffic drops shrink httpevent baselines and can look like routing loss when applications genuinely idled.
